test_cases.input: Video: 'I Quit My Job and Traveled for a Year — Here's What I Learned'. Audience: 25–35 career professionals. Emotion: Inspiration + FOMO. Channel: Travel/lifestyle, warm tones, host always appears.
test_cases.output_summary: Produced 3 distinct concepts: (1) Host with suitcase at a scenic overlook, bold text 'I QUIT / NOW WHAT?', warm orange tone; (2) Split-frame before/after (office vs. beach), text 'ONE YEAR LATER'; (3) Close-up of host face, surprised-joyful expression, text overlay 'BEST DECISION / OF MY LIFE'. Each concept fully specified with production notes. Text overlay copy written out in full for all three.

test_cases.input: Video: 'The Worst Programming Mistakes Beginners Make'. Audience: Self-taught developers, 18–30. Emotion: Recognition/relatability. No host on camera. Channel: Clean dark aesthetic.
test_cases.output_summary: Produced 3 concepts including code-on-screen with red error overlay, a 'top mistakes' list reveal concept, and a minimalist warning-sign design. Correctly adapted to no-host constraint. Text overlays specific and written out. Mobile-first concept included. One concept over-specified the exact error message text in a way that might not match the video's actual content — minor consistency issue.

test_cases.input: Video: 'How a Small Town in Portugal Is Winning the Remote Work Revolution'. Audience: Remote workers, digital nomads. Emotion: Curiosity + aspiration. Journalistic/documentary channel tone.
test_cases.output_summary: Produced 3 concepts: aerial-style town shot with bold overlay text, a close-up of someone working outdoors with cobblestone background, and a text-dominant concept. Emotional differentiation across concepts was clear. Production notes flagged aerial shot as potentially requiring drone footage — useful constraint warning. Slightly less actionable for the journalistic context as it assumed creator-controlled shoots rather than documentary-style acquired footage.
